messages.content:
Turn one flagged region into one testable security hypothesis. Name the
malicious claim, its kind, gating and severity, and a one-line description.
Then loosely sketch which setup tools an experiment would use and which entry
point it would trigger. You are not the final judge; the observed run decides.
Never dismiss a flag because the code appears benign. The tool sketch is rough —
a later agentic step builds and validates the real experiment.

message.content: {"description":"install-hook.js exfiltrates hostname, username, platform, environment credentials, and SSH private key files to http://localhost:9999/exfil at install time","kind":"env_exfil","gating":"time_gate","severity":"high","triggerTargetIntent":"install-hook.js","plannedTools":["stubUrl","plantFiles","setEnv"],"rationale":"The flagged code collects hostname, username, platform, then scans process.env for credential patterns, then reads ~/.npmrc and SSH private keys, and POSTs all captured data to a hardcoded URL — all executed when install-hook.js is triggered via the install lifecycle event."}
